name: Faith Cooperative
description_short: Faith Cooperative Federal Credit Union is a Dallas-based credit union offering checking, savings, auto loans, and online banking services to members in the Dallas, TX area and beyond.
description_long:
Faith Cooperative Federal Credit Union operates as a federally-chartered credit union serving the Dallas, Texas community. The organization is regulated by the NCUA (National Credit Union Administration) and maintains membership accounts across multiple service areas. Based on available NCUA data, the credit union maintains a modest but active membership base with specific account holdings across various product lines.

Faith Cooperative offers a comprehensive suite of consumer financial products including high-yield checking and savings accounts, share certificates (CDs), auto loans (both new and used), payday alternative loans, and personal loans. Members have access to robust online and mobile banking platforms with features including online loan payments, e-statements, and external account transfers. The credit union also provides financial literacy workshops and dedicated financial planning services to support member financial education.

As a credit union, Faith Cooperative operates on a member-owned cooperative model rather than shareholder profit maximization, which theoretically aligns incentives with member financial wellbeing. The organization advertises competitive auto loan rates (2.39% for new vehicles, 2.70% for used) and offers payday alternative loans as safer alternatives to traditional payday lending. Members benefit from exclusive discounts and a branch locator system for in-person service access.

The credit union serves a relatively small membership base, with NCUA data showing 968 active share savings accounts, 11 share certificates, and notably 0 checking accounts reported—a significant data point suggesting either underreporting, recent account classification changes, or limited checking account adoption. Average auto loan amounts of $22,000 and membership of 10+ new auto loan holders and 60+ used auto loan holders indicates a modest loan portfolio. The organization's modest size means fewer resources and branch locations compared to large regional banks, which may limit convenience for some members. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Share Savings Accounts (Regular savings with competitive interest rates)
Share Certificates/CDs (Low-minimum balance certificates with higher rates)
Share Draft Checking Accounts (Debit cards, checks, ATM access)
New Auto Loans (Financing for new vehicles at 2.39% APR)
Used Auto Loans (Financing for pre-owned vehicles at 2.70% APR)
Payday Alternative Loans (Small emergency loans under 36% APR)
Online Banking Platform (Bill pay, account management, transfers)
E-Statements (Electronic account statements and paperless access)
Online Loan Payments (Secure payment processing for existing loans)
External Account Transfers (Funds transfer to accounts at other institutions)
Financial Literacy Workshops (Member education on financial concepts)
pros: Payday Alternative Loans (PALs) offered as safer alternative to traditional payday lending with lower rates
Competitive auto loan rates starting at 2.39% APR for new vehicles and 2.70% for used vehicles
Member-owned cooperative structure aligns organizational incentives with member financial wellbeing
Robust online and mobile banking platform with online payments, e-statements, and external transfers
Financial literacy workshops and dedicated financial planning services for members
Share Certificates (CDs) available with competitive rates for long-term savings goals
Comprehensive account types including high-yield checking and savings options
cons: Extremely small membership base (968 savings accounts, 11 CDs, 0 checking accounts reported) limits resources and service expansion
NCUA data shows zero checking accounts despite advertising Share Draft checking accounts, suggesting data inconsistency or very low adoption
Limited geographic footprint restricted primarily to Dallas, Texas area unlike larger regional credit unions
Modest loan portfolio (10+ new auto loans, 60+ used auto loans) may indicate limited lending capacity or underwriting flexibility
No information available about credit card offerings, business lending, or investment advisory services beyond wealth management mentions
best_for: Dallas-area residents seeking credit union membership with member-owned cooperative benefits
Borrowers looking for payday alternative loans with rates under 36% APR
Auto buyers seeking competitive rates on new or used vehicle financing
Members prioritizing online and mobile banking convenience with solid digital tools
diagnosis: Faith Cooperative FCU is best for Dallas-area residents and existing members seeking basic consumer banking services, auto financing, and payday alternative loans through a member-owned cooperative structure. The primary caveat is the credit union's very modest membership base and apparent data inconsistencies (zero reported checking accounts despite offering them), which may indicate operational constraints or reporting issues that consumers should verify directly before joining.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
